title: haworthiopsis viscosa var. beanii
intro: Haworthiopsis viscosa var. beanii, also known as the sticky Haworthia or Bean's Haworthia, is a rare and unique succulent species that belongs to the Asphodelaceae family. This plant is prized for its small, triangular leaves that have a sticky, resinous coating, which is thought to have medicinal properties. The resin of Haworthiopsis viscosa var. beanii has been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's resin is often used to make salves, ointments, and infusions that are believed to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
